key_insight: The paper introduces a way to build 1-Lipschitz neural networks directly on Hadamard manifolds by using Busemann-function-based gradient-descent layers that preserve geometry and stability.
engineering_relevance: This provides a principled toolkit for robust learning on curved spaces such as hyperbolic embeddings and SPD matrices, where Euclidean Lipschitz controls do not apply.
method: Construct 1-Lipschitz layers via Busemann gradient flows on Hadamard manifolds.
Use quasi-α-firmly nonexpansive, gradient-descent-type updates to preserve nonexpansiveness.
Instantiate the framework on the Poincaré disk and SPD manifold for classification and denoising tasks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports two evaluations: robust classification on the Poincaré disk under hyperbolic perturbations and masked-Wishart covariance reconstruction on SPD matrices, where the nonexpansive denoiser outperforms several baselines.
watch_item: Performance and convergence depend on manifold-specific constructions, so implementing the layers correctly on a target geometry may be nontrivial.
